title: Use correct predicates for validity threats
content: Bind validity threats using orkgp:P39099 instead of inventing predicates like pgmr:classifies_threat_to_validity.
avoid: Do not use incorrect or invented predicates for validity threats.

title: Use correct nested paths for data collection methods
content: For data collection questions, bind ?contribution -> orkgp:P56008 -> orkgp:P1005 for methods.
avoid: Do not skip parent nodes when asking for nested data collection fields.

title: Use correct nested paths for data analysis metrics
content: For data analysis questions, bind ?contribution -> orkgp:P15124 -> orkgp:P57016 for machine learning metrics.
avoid: Do not skip parent nodes when asking for nested data analysis fields.

title: Use correct nested paths for descriptive statistics
content: For descriptive statistics, bind ?contribution -> orkgp:P15124 -> orkgp:P56048 for measures of central tendency.
avoid: Do not skip parent nodes when asking for nested descriptive statistics fields.

title: Bind specific metrics instead of using generic placeholders
content: For metrics like accuracy, bind specific nodes instead of using generic placeholders like pgmr:data_analysis/pgmr:metric.
avoid: Do not use generic placeholders for specific metrics.

title: Use label filters for specific validity threats
content: When filtering for validity threats, use specific labels like pgmr:construct_validity instead of generic mentions.
avoid: Do not use generic mentions for filtering validity threats.

title: Use COUNT for distinct counts of papers
content: For counting distinct papers, use COUNT(DISTINCT ?paper) instead of incorrect counting methods.
avoid: Do not use incorrect counting methods for distinct papers.

title: Use FILTER for specific conditions
content: Use FILTER for specific conditions like publication year instead of incorrect predicates.
avoid: Do not use incorrect predicates for filtering conditions.
